presentation.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Your First Business Tool

slides.title: Day 12 — Your First Business Tool
slides.body: Every entrepreneur needs a way to diagnose customer problems.
slides.speakerNotes:
Open: Yesterday you saw the opportunity. Today you get your first business tool.
Not paperwork. A diagnostic instrument.
discussionQuestions: What tools do other businesses use to understand customers?

slides.title: How Businesses Diagnose Problems
slides.body:
Restaurants taste food.
Doctors diagnose patients.
Mechanics inspect vehicles.
Financial Advisors conduct an FNA.
slides.speakerNotes:
Walk through each example.
Emphasize: professionals diagnose before they prescribe.
FNA is the advisor's diagnostic — a business tool.
discussionQuestions: What happens when a business skips diagnosis?

slides.title: FNA — Business Tool, Not Paperwork
slides.speakerNotes:
Reframe FNA entirely.
No product language.
This is how your venture understands client needs before creating value.

slides.title: Ethical Boundaries
slides.body: No product talk until needs are documented.
slides.speakerNotes:
Entrepreneurs who rush to sell lose trust.
Advisors who rush to products lose clients.

slides.title: Deck 01 Close — FNA Workshop
slides.body: Afternoon: practice diagnosis with real discovery discipline.
